reviews.review_title: BEST FIRST DAY I'VE EVER HAD!!!
reviews.review_description: I was really looking for a good bird game, until I found this! My search for bird games was finally over. And my first day was amazing!!! I immediately learned how to fly properly all on my own, and I talked and had a good time with some people–but best of all, I MET THE OWNER! and he was really kind, and we chatted for what felt like forever! We talked about the game, ideas for it, and just played around and had a good time! Best day I've ever had in a GOOD while. Thank you!

developer_response.body: Thank YOU, star!!

reviews.review_title: This game is the best ever
reviews.review_description: I love this game so much that it's almost as good as jumping out of a plane or using a squirrel suit. It's awesome. I don't understand how it could have any bad reviews u less. The bad review is because the person is too lazy to flap their arms and get a workout while having fun.  Your game is awesome. Keep it going, add more levels, or make the levels bigger, but it doesn't matter. The game is awesome, and I'd recommend it to everyone

developer_response.body: you're the GOAT! Thank you!

reviews.review_description:
Exer Gale is one of those rare VR experiences that somehow manages to feel both chaotic and cozy at the same time. From the moment you put on your headset, you're dropped into a world that just gets it.
The cosmetics alone make a strong first impression. Wings, masks, tattoos, the level of customization is genuinely impressive. It's not just picking colors, it's an actual expression. Seeing another player's choice of cosmetics mid-match and wondering how they pulled that look together never gets old.
The Tag gamemode is pure, distilled VR fun. There's something about physically weaving through the air, finding shortcuts and outsmarting your opponents that no flatscreen game can replicate. The tension of being hunted, or the thrill of being the hunter, hits completely different in VR.
If Tag is the adrenaline rush, Collectathon is the breather. It scratches a quieter itch, methodical, competitive in its own way, and oddly satisfying. The two modes complement each other perfectly.
None of this would land as hard without the music and aesthetics tying everything together. The visual style is cohesive and charming, and the soundtrack genuinely enhances the experience rather than just filling silence.
What really earns trust though is the dev team. Updates drop consistently, feedback gets heard, and there's always something new to look forward to, whether that's fresh cosmetics or new content.
If you have a Meta Quest and you're not playing Exer Gale, fix that.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the in-depth review! \o/

reviews.review_title: The best game I ever played on VR!
reviews.review_description: It's really fun and good for the times when you have a lot of power. There's a lot of beautiful locations and they all have beautiful places. It feels like I'm in a dream there  because I always wanted to fly. I give it a 5 stars for a reason.

reviews.review_description: Absolutely fantastic game, my daughter loves it, really immersive and surprisingly good for burning calories as you have to use your arms to fly like a bird. And it's completely free! Sure there are in game purchases however these are optional and cheap. The multiplayer is very interactive also

reviews.review_title: Awesome mecanics! game could be better.
reviews.review_description: I personally LOVE the flight mechanics of this game and often play purely for the satisfying illusion of flight. However, the actual  gameplay could be better.  currently the only playstyle is the timed flight levels, which is ok, but they arent much for multiplayer and your highscores are only saved for a week. I think it would be fun to have some kind of gamemodes for the lobby, like tag, racing, or some kind of ball game. I also think there should be overall highscores that show the best of all time. Another thing i think could be cool is in-game currency, but i think that's a whole other thing of its own. If i have more ideas i might return and say them, but overall i ADORE this game and cant wait to see it continue to grow!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the awesome review! We are currently in the process of introducing a new gamemode to the game that should add more variety. And we have plans for a soft currency system. 🙂

reviews.review_description: Birb

I had the pleasure of playing Exer Gale in closed alpha. Now that it's available on the Meta Horizon Store. I'd highly recommend downloading Exer Gale. The flying mechanics are absolutely amazing. The visual and sound design are incredible and all elements combined really contribute to the overall aesthetics of the game.
Amazing work done by the developers of Exer Gale.

developer_response.body: Thank you for your support! We really do appreciate it! 🙏

reviews.review_description: I've played a couple of different 'flap your hands to fly' but this one is incredible! Not only can you fly but landing on the ground feels great (you keep a bit of your speed) and you can use the stick to move around or push yourself by flapping. It's also got grip climbing and feels really polished. This isn't your typical free to play game, the free version gives you a lot but the full experience is behind a pay wall, but I'm so glad the devs chose to let people play a bunch of it for free because EVERYONE NEEDS TO EXPERIENCE WHAT IT FEELS LIKE TO FLAP YOUR HANDS AND FLY. And the art and music is gorgeous. I love how landing on the water bounces you along. The game is single player but gives you the option for multiplayer

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We really appreciate it! 🙏

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review. We are currently working on a new gamemode that should bring a bit more spice. And there are some free cosmetics that we add from time to time. Hope this helps 🙏

developer_response.body: If flight controls were too easy, you are an exceptional player! Because most people need a Tutorial to get used to the wings. Thank you for trying, and we hope you will change the rating in the future! - Exer Gale Team

reviews.review_title: 🕊️ Feel the Wind — Fly in VR
reviews.review_description:
Finally, a VR game where you can truly fly like a bird and live out your Icarus dreams. The sensation of flight is wonderfully captured — soaring through the sky, catching the wind, gently landing, and taking off with a swift motion of your wings.

The execution is solid and immersive, with a surprising amount of depth. It’s not as easy as it seems — you’ll need practice, timing, and a good eye for wind currents to master your flight path. There’s plenty of content to explore, making it a refreshing and unique addition to any VR library.

If you’ve ever dreamed of flying, this one delivers.

I just wasted all my money on premium because I thought I was going to get a lot of stuff but I didn't, all I got was a stupid key I don't even know what to do with it

Premium was so expensive at least give access to all maps.

developer_response.body: Hey jarjar21112022! Thank you for playing Exer Gale! Currently, the premium maps include Claws of Garuda, Dark Gorge, Rising Crown, and Rocky Islands. All maps in Exer Gale can be unlocked through single-player mode.

The developers promise what they will be.

developer_response.body: Happy to make the dream a reality! More content is coming soon! 🙏

reviews.review_title: Wish I could fly like that in real life
reviews.review_description: I've never seen such well executed flying mechanics combined with the best workout I've ever had while playing a game. This game might benefit from something like floating sea mines that you could bounce off to gain speed, just a suggestion.

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We'll keep it in mind 😉

reviews.review_description:
I've played several VR games over the years that simulate flying like a bird, (Eagle Flight on PSVR1, and RUSH and Zenith on Quest), and Exer Gale has the best flight mechanics.

It's a real joy to fly in Exer Gale.  The sense of speed is intense.

It doesn't make a good first impression though.

You start off in a bland cave which immediately set off "weekend indie project" alarm bells.

The menu is really janky and difficult to use.  You have to poke the buttons with your finger but the fingertip doesn't stop on the button, it goes right through so you have to very precisely press it.

The strangest thing though is despite the game rendering in stereoscopic 3D, it looks flat. I had to test for parallax by closing one eye at a time to see if it really was stereoscopic.  I don't know why it looks so flat, it's very off putting, but once I got into the flying I had fun.  Possibly adding more contrast and shadows and/or increasing the stereoscopic effect would give it more depth.

Multiplayer took a few tries to connect and once it finally connected I was the only one there.

I'm not sure how much potential this game has because I think the thrill of flying will wear off quickly if all there is to do are timed item collection races.

Despite this after my first play I immediately purchased the Premium Edition. I don't know what the developer's plans are for the future but I hope they can do something more with this great flight model.

For example I'd love to play an adventure game, single player or co-op, as this creature.

developer_response.body:
Hi Jioxx, thanks a lot for your detailed review!

We’re really happy you enjoyed the flight mechanics. This was one of our main focuses before launching in Early Access.

As for the other issues you mentioned, we're actively working on them and hope to improve things throughout the Early Access phase.

Thanks again for supporting us! If you have any more feedback, feel free to join the Exer Gale Community Discord.

Exer Gale Team

reviews.review_description: This game is a great arm workout and the flying feels real. I don't know if I messed something up, but at first it was telling me to download an app to save my game play. I dismissed it at first since I just wanted to try it out. Now I want to save my game play and I can't find the app to download in Google play and the option to save no longer pops up. I can't access a menu in the game to find other options either...

developer_response.body:
Exer Gale is not published on Google Play, and we currently have no plans to release it as a mobile app. For saving your gameplay, please contact Meta Support, as they are responsible for Meta-related apps.
– The Exer Gale Team

reviews.review_title: Best VR Flying I've ever seen!
reviews.review_description: Exer Gale is such a fun and engaging game, the level of quality put into the artstyle and gameplay is something you really don't see done so well on indie VR projects. This game is really high on replayability, enjoyability, fair/balanced gameplay, and fun physical challenges, and low on frustration and boredom. I'd heavily recommend check out and buying the full release!!

developer_response.body: Thank you for the review! We tried hard to give this game it's own identity and feel, glad to see it paid off! 😁
